Short Title, subtitle C, , , which is classified generally to this subchapter, is popularly known as the swampbuster provisions.Pub. L. 99–198, title XII99 Stat. 1507Dec. 23, 1985

children.text: in violation of this section; and
ineligible for loans or payments in an amount determined by the Secretary to be proportionate to the severity of the violation.

children.heading: Ineligibility for certain loans and payments
children.label: b Ineligibility for certain loans and payments

children.text: Contract payments under a production flexibility contract, marketing assistance loans, and any type of price support or payment made available under the Agricultural Market Transition Act [ et seq.], the Commodity Credit Corporation Charter Act ( et seq.), or any other Act.7 U.S.C. 720115 U.S.C. 714

A loan made or guaranteed under the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act ( et seq.) or any other provision of law administered by the Consolidated Farm Service Agency, if the Secretary determines that the proceeds of the loan will be used for a purpose that will contribute to conversion of a wetland (other than as provided in this subchapter) to produce an agricultural commodity.7 U.S.C. 19211See Change of Name note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: A payment made pursuant to a contract entered into under the environmental quality incentives program under subpart A of part IV of subchapter IV.

children.label: B
children.text: A payment under any other provision of subchapter IV.

children.label: C
children.text: A payment under section 2201 or 2202 of this title.

children.label: D
children.text: A payment, loan, or other assistance under section 1003 or 1006a of this title.

children.heading: Ineligibility for crop insurance premium assistance
children.label: c Ineligibility for crop insurance premium assistance

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a person is determined to have committed a violation under subsection (a) or (d) during a crop year, the person shall be ineligible to receive any payment of any portion of premium paid by the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation for a plan or policy of insurance under the Federal Crop Insurance Act ( et seq.) pursuant to this subsection.7 U.S.C. 1501

children.label: i
children.text: only apply to reinsurance years subsequent to the date of a final determination of a violation, including all administrative appeals; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not apply to the existing reinsurance year or any reinsurance year prior to the date of the final determination.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), ineligibility for crop insurance premium assistance shall apply in accordance with this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: the person shall be ineligible to receive crop insurance premium subsidies in subsequent reinsurance years unless the Secretary determines that an exemption pursuant to applies; orsection 3822 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: for any violation that the Secretary determines impacts less than 5 acres of an entire farm, the person may pay a contribution in an amount equal to 150 percent of the cost of mitigation, as determined by the Secretary, to the fund described in for wetland restoration in lieu of ineligibility to receive crop insurance premium assistance.section 3841(f) of this title

children.heading: Prior conversions
children.label: C Prior conversions
children.text: In the case of a wetland that the Secretary determines was converted prior to , ineligibility under this subsection shall not apply.February 7, 2014

children.heading: Conversions and new policies or plans of insurance
children.label: D Conversions and new policies or plans of insurance
i
children.text: ineligibility shall apply only to conversions that take place after the date on which the policy or plan of insurance first becomes available to the person; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the person shall take such steps as the Secretary determines appropriate to mitigate any prior conversion in a timely manner but not to exceed 2 reinsurance years.

children.heading: Mitigation required
children.label: A Mitigation required
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, a person subject to a final determination, including all administrative appeals, of a violation described in subsection (d) shall have 1 reinsurance year to initiate a mitigation plan to remedy the violation, as determined by the Secretary, before becoming ineligible under this subsection in the following reinsurance year to receive any payment of any portion of the premium paid by the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation for a policy or plan of insurance under the Federal Crop Insurance Act ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 1501

children.heading: Persons covered for the first time
children.label: B Persons covered for the first time
children.text: Notwithstanding the requirements of paragraph (1), in the case of a person that is subject to this subsection for the first time solely due to the amendment made by section 2611(b) of the Agricultural Act of 2014, the person shall have 2 reinsurance years after the reinsurance year in which a final determination is made, including all administrative appeals, of a violation described in this subsection to take such steps as the Secretary determines appropriate to remedy or mitigate the violation in accordance with this subsection.

children.heading: Good faith
children.label: C Good faith
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a person subject to a final determination, including all administrative appeals, of a violation described in this subsection acted in good faith and without intent to commit a violation described in this subsection as described in , the person shall have 2 reinsurance years to take such steps as the Secretary determines appropriate to remedy or mitigate the violation in accordance with this subsection.section 3822(h) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the tenant has made a good faith effort to meet the requirements of this section, including enlisting the assistance of the Secretary to obtain a reasonable plan for restoration or mitigation for the farm;

children.label: II
children.text: the landlord on the farm refuses to comply with the plan on the farm; and

children.label: III
children.text: the Secretary determines that the lack of compliance is not a part of a scheme or device to avoid the compliance.

children.heading: Report
children.label: ii Report
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate an annual report concerning the ineligibility determinations limited during the previous 12-month period under this subparagraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Beginning with the first full reinsurance year immediately following , all persons seeking eligibility for the payment of a portion of the premium paid by the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation for a policy or plan of insurance under the Federal Crop Insurance Act ( et seq.) shall provide certification of compliance with this section as determined by the Secretary.February 7, 20147 U.S.C. 1501

children.label: I
children.text: a person who has properly complied with certification shall be held harmless with regard to eligibility during the period of evaluation; and

children.label: II
children.text: if the Secretary fails to evaluate the certification in a timely manner and the person is subsequently found to be in violation of this subsection, ineligibility shall not apply to the person for that violation.

children.label: aa
children.text: determine the amount of an equitable contribution to conservation by the person for the violation; and

children.label: bb
children.text: deposit the contribution in the fund described in .section 3841(f) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: II Limitation
children.text: The contribution shall not exceed the total of the portion of the premium paid by the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation for a policy or plan of insurance for all years the person is determined to have been in violation subsequent to the date on which certification was first required under this subparagraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary shall use existing processes and procedures for certifying compliance.

children.heading: Responsibility
children.label: B Responsibility
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the agencies of the Department of Agriculture, shall be solely responsible for determining whether a producer is eligible to receive crop insurance premium subsidies in accordance with this subsection.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that no agent, approved insurance provider, or employee or contractor of an agency or approved insurance provider, bears responsibility or liability for the eligibility of an insured producer under this subsection, other than in cases of misrepresentation, fraud, or scheme and device.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in and notwithstanding any other provision of law, any person who in any crop year beginning after , converts a wetland by draining, dredging, filling, leveling, or any other means for the purpose, or to have the effect, of making the production of an agricultural commodity possible on such converted wetland shall be ineligible for those payments, loans, or programs specified in subsection (b) for that crop year and all subsequent crop years.section 3822 of this titleNovember 28, 1990

children.heading: Duty of the Secretary
children.label: 2 Duty of the Secretary
children.text: No person shall become ineligible under paragraph (1) if the Secretary determines that an exemption under applies to that person.section 3822(b) of this title

children.heading: Prior loans
children.label: e Prior loans
children.text: This section shall not apply to a loan described in subsection (b) made before .December 23, 1985

children.heading: Wetland
children.label: f Wetland
children.text: The Secretary shall have, and shall not delegate to any private person or entity, authority to determine whether a person has complied with this subchapter.
